svg.text: MONGOLIAN (монгол хэл) / KAZAKH (қазақ тілі)
MONGOLIC / TURKIC · EURASIAN STEPPE · BORDERING PEOPLES
PLATE 57A ALT · SCRIPT & SOUNDS
— MONGOLIAN: TWO SCRIPTS IN PARALLEL —
Traditional Mongolian Script (ᠤᠠᠨᠭᠠᠯ ᠪᠢᠴᠢᠭ): vertical, read top-to-bottom, left-to-right
Derived from Old Uyghur script (which came from Sogdian, which came from Aramaic)
Letters change form depending on position (initial/medial/final) in a word
Inner Mongolia (China): traditional script officially in use alongside Chinese
MONGOLIAN CYRILLIC (SINCE 1941)
35 letters: 33 Russian Cyrillic + 2 added (Ө /ø/ and Ү /y/) for Mongolian sounds
Mongolia (nation): Cyrillic standard since 1941 under Soviet influence
2020: Mongolia announced revival of traditional script alongside Cyrillic
MONGOLIAN CONSONANTS & VOWELS
Consonants: /p b t d ts dz k g/ · /m n ŋ/ · /f s ʃ x h/ · /r l j w/
7 vowels: /a e i o u ø y/ — with Cyrillic: a э и o у ө ү
VOWEL HARMONY: back vowels (a o u) vs front vowels (э ө ү) in each word
VOWEL HARMONY IN MONGOLIAN
Back-vowel word: газар /ɡa.zər/ (gazar) = land/place
Front-vowel word: түмэн /tø.mən/ (tumen) = 10,000 / abyss
Suffixes harmonize: -t (+back) vs -эд (+front) = same grammatical function
MONGOLIAN TRADITIONAL SCRIPT SAMPLE
ᠤᠠᠨᠭᠠᠯ ᠪᠢᠴᠢᠭ = "Mongolian language" (traditional script, read vertically)
монгол хэл = mongol khel (Cyrillic) = Mongolian language
SAMPLE WORDS (CYRILLIC)
сайн байна (sain baina uu?) = hello (lit. "are you well?")
баярла (bayarlaa) = thank you · ус (us) = water
KAZAKH (қазақ тілі) — TURKIC · STEPPE NEIGHBOR
— KAZAKH: THREE SCRIPTS, ONE LANGUAGE —
Kazakh has been written in THREE different scripts in the modern era:
1. Arabic script (traditional; still used by Kazakhs in China/Xinjiang)
2. Cyrillic (Soviet period, 1940–present; still primary in Kazakhstan)
3. Latin (announced 2017; transition ongoing; target: all by 2031)
KAZAKH CYRILLIC: 42 LETTERS
33 Russian Cyrillic + 9 added: ә ғ қ ң ө ү ұ і һ
Extra letters mark sounds not in Russian: /q/ /ғ/ /ŋ/ /ɢ/ /ɣ/ /ħ/ etc.
KAZAKH LATIN (CURRENT TRANSITION VERSION)
Uses apostrophe-based notation: Aʼ, Oʼ, Uʼ, Yʼ, Gʼ, Nʼ for special sounds
Controversial: apostrophes in every other word impede readability
Revised versions proposed; final form not yet settled (as of 2026)
KAZAKH VOWEL HARMONY (9 VOWELS)
Back: a o u ы ә (ә = schwa) · Front: е ө ү і
Suffixes harmonize: -lar (back) vs -ler (front) for plural
ат /at/ (horse) → ат-тар (horses) · үй /øj/ (house) → үй-лер (houses)
SAMPLE WORDS (KAZAKH CYRILLIC)
Сәлеметсіз бе? (салеметсіз бе?) = hello (are you well?)
рахмет (rakhmet) = thank you · су (su) = water
Both languages greet with "are you well?" — steppe hospitality encoded in grammar
